parent_scalar_context.git_head: db616ea Record G900 six-nine fork plateau checkpoint
interpretation_boundary: This audit finds a pre-receipt source-side asymmetry inside the observed [9,11] fork.
It does not derive why the source construction produces outside=3, inside=6/9, anchor=9/6, or root=12.
It does not prove a broad nearest-anchor law beyond the sole observed fork.
No closure witness, transport relation, channel, force, physics, GR, Maxwell, QCD, electroweak, or unification claim is made.
parent_scalar_context.question: Before receipt or registry lookup, does the six-nine [9,11] fork contain a source-side asymmetry distinguishing the receipt branch?
parent_scalar_context.reading: A pre-receipt source-side asymmetry is present inside the six-nine [9,11] fork. The receipt branch has ordered ladder [3,6,9,12] with equal deltas [3,3,3], and its anchor is the nearest endpoint to source root 12. The absent branch uses the same value set but the order [3,9,6,12] breaks that clean ladder. This is a solid native-source hold, but it does not yet derive why the source construction produces these values.
solid_hold_summary: receipt branch ladder [3,6,9,12] is sorted and strictly ascending
receipt branch ladder linear deltas are [3,3,3]
receipt branch ladder mod15 deltas are [3,3,3]
receipt branch anchor 9 is nearest to source root 12
absent branch has same value set but order [3,9,6,12], breaking the clean ladder
